prompt_preview: /ulw compare server-session auth vs JWT and recommend one
note: compare+recommend reads as advisory despite /ulw prefix; under /ulw a paired compare+recommend is plausibly execution but the second verb (recommend) carries advisory flavor. Captured here so the regression net does NOT pin this behavior; classifier evolution is free to reclassify.

bare 'stop' as a single token is ambiguous — could be session_management ('stop the session') or execution ('stop the running process'). Currently routes execution. Captured here so the regression net does NOT pin this behavior.

prompt_preview: can you check on the status of the deploy
note: polite-form question without trailing '?' classifies as execution. The polite-imperative branch lacks a '?$' disqualifier (deferred F-002 — adding it regresses '/ulw could you fix this?' to advisory which is wrong under /ulw). Captured here as known acceptable behavior under /ulw, suspected misclassification outside /ulw context.

prompt_preview: We have a deadlock between sales and marketing teams
note: v1.27.0 metaphor-misroute: 'deadlock' is in the new architecture-vocab list, so this metaphorical use scores +1 on coding even though context is org-design. Domain SHOULD be general here; stored=coding reflects the current (buggy) classification. A promotion fires only when a future classifier rev disambiguates metaphors. Real-world /ulw users rarely hit this; documented to acknowledge the trade-off. Mitigation would require non-coding-noun gating which is overengineering.

prompt_preview: Lets put a circuit breaker on the office HVAC
note: v1.27.0 metaphor-misroute: 'circuit breaker' (engineering pattern) coincides with literal-electrical-engineering use. Same trade-off as the deadlock metaphor row above. Stored=coding reflects current behavior; should be general.
